zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard Ince於大約1285年出生在英國藍卡爾維根時，他的父親Alfredus De Haydock26歲，母親Alesia Alice Standish21歲。他和Elizabeth Radcliffe至少有2個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於大約1333年在英國英格蘭爾因斯享年50歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Richard Ince于大约1285年出生在英国藍卡爾維根时，他的父亲Alfredus De Haydock26岁，母亲Alesia Alice Standish21岁。他和Elizabeth Radcliffe至少有2个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于大约1333年在英国英格兰爾因斯享年50岁。

nameMeanings.name: Ince
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: habitational name from Ince in Makerfield (Lancashire), Ince (Cheshire), or Ince Blundell (Lancashire). The placenames derive from Proto-Welsh ïnïs ‘island’.

possibly a post-medieval West Midlands variant of Hints, a habitational name from either of two places called Hints (Staffordshire, Shropshire). The placenames probably derive from Proto-Welsh hïnt ‘way, path’ + Middle English plural -es.

possibly a variant with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s of Inn, from residence at an inn or inns, from Middle English in (singular) or innes (plural) ‘tavern, lodging(s), tenement(s)’. There seems also to have been some confusion with Scottish Innes .

variants.name: Ence
meanings: Americanized form of South German   Enz  , a short form of   Anselm  .
History: Two brothers, Johannes and Gottlieb Enz, immigrated from northern Switzerland to southern Utah around 1860. Their descendants bear the surname Ence.

variants.name: Innes
meanings: Scottish: shortened form of McInnis , from Gaelic Mag Aonghuis ‘son of Aonghus’.
Scottish: habitational name from the barony of Innes in Urquhart (Morayshire), named from Gaelic inis ‘island’.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard Ince was born about 1285, in Wigan, Lancashire, England, his father, Alfredus De Haydock, was 26 and his mother, Alesia Alice Standish, was 21. He had at least 2 sons and 1 daughter with Elizabeth Radcliffe. He died about 1333, in Ince, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 50.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard Ince est né environ 1285, à Wigan, Lancashire, Angleterre, son père, Alfredus De Haydock, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alesia Alice Standish, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 1 fille avec Elizabeth Radcliffe. Il est décédé environ 1333, à Ince, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 50 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Richard Ince um 1285 in Wigan, Lancashire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Alfredus De Haydock 26 und seine Mutter Alesia Alice Standish 21. Er hatte mit Elizabeth Radcliffe mindestens 2 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1333 im Alter von 50 Jahren in Ince, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Richard Ince è nato il circa 1285 nel Wigan, Lancashire, England, il suo padre, Alfredus De Haydock, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Alesia Alice Standish, aveva 21 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 1 figlia con Elizabeth Radcliffe. Morì circa 1333, nel Ince, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 50 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Richard Ince(이)가 대략 1285년 영국 랭카실르 위건에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Alfredus De Haydock(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Alesia Alice Standish(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Elizabeth Radcliffe(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1333년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 인스에서 50세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Richard Ince родился около 1285 г. в местности виган, ланкашир, Англия, его отцу по имени Alfredus De Haydock было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alesia Alice Standish, 21.
